name: Coupon Redemption at Checkout
description: Customer-facing coupon code entry on the pricing/checkout flow, with server-authoritative validation and discount application (expiry, per-user/total caps, enterprise-only, first-purchase-only, minimum-purchase, abuse rate-limiting).

frontend.evidence: frontend/index.html:5985-5991 renders a 'Have a coupon code?' toggle, a coupon-code input, and an Apply button on the pricing section; frontend/index.html:11026-11089 (cdbToggleCouponInput/cdbApplyCoupon) sends the code as coupon_code on order creation and surfaces validation errors so a bad/expired/exhausted coupon does not silently fall through — verified 2026-07-08.

navigation.evidence: Embedded directly in the public pricing section of frontend/index.html, which is the platform's primary landing page.

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/couponSystem.test.mjs exercises the redemption engine in workers/src/lib/coupons.js directly (confirmed by file presence and naming; import statement matches the module under test).

verification.evidence: workers/src/lib/coupons.js (16 exported functions incl. 8 handleAdmin* — see CAP-BILL-002) read directly; workers/src/handlers/payments.js:83 handleCreateOrder confirmed to accept an unauthenticated authCtx ({} default) so anonymous checkout with a coupon is possible; frontend/index.html:5985-5991,11026-11089 read directly.

notes: customer_journey_complete is false only because no dynamic_browser (real click-through) pass has been run yet, not because of a known defect — static evidence for this capability is unusually strong (server-authoritative validation, real UI, real tests). A dynamic_browser pass is the natural next step to upgrade this to a fully verified GA claim.

name: Coupon Administration
description: Staff creation, editing, activation/deactivation, and redemption-audit of discount coupons.

frontend.evidence: Case-insensitive search for the string 'coupon' in frontend/admin-portal.html and frontend/ops-dashboard.html — the platform's two staff/admin consoles — returns zero matches. No page anywhere calls any /api/admin/coupons* route. Matches docs/SAAS_PRODUCTIZATION_MISSION_BRIEF.md:234 Gap Matrix row (Domain 11): 'Coupons admin: Exists, no UI (API-only)', independently re-confirmed 2026-07-08.

navigation.evidence: No nav entry anywhere links to a coupon-admin page, because no coupon-admin page exists.

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/couponSystem.test.mjs — same test file as CAP-BILL-001; covers the workers/src/lib/coupons.js module the handleAdmin* functions live in.

verification.evidence: workers/src/lib/coupons.js:378,389,399,456,514,530,562,574 each read directly — every handleAdmin* function opens with `if (authCtx?.isAdmin !== true) return fail(request, 'Admin access required', 403, 'ADMIN_ONLY')`, the legacy isAdmin flag rather than the newer permission registry in workers/src/auth/rbac.js, but a real, enforced gate. workers/src/index.js:2930-2984 grepped for /api/admin/coupons* route registrations; frontend/admin-portal.html and frontend/ops-dashboard.html grepped case-insensitively for 'coupon' (0 matches).

notes: Authorization is real (isAdmin-gated on every handler) — the gap is purely the missing UI. Whoever builds the admin UI for this should consider migrating the gate to workers/src/auth/rbac.js's admin:business:read/admin:infra:operate permissions for consistency with CAP-RBAC-001, but that is a cleanup opportunity, not a defect.

name: Subscription Plans & Billing Portal
description: View current plan/usage, upgrade/downgrade subscription, view invoices and payment history, and receive payment-failure notifications.

frontend.evidence: frontend/billing-portal.html (563 lines) implements cancel/upgrade/usage/invoices; reachable from frontend/user-dashboard.html:2769, which navigates the browser to that page, wired to the sidebar's Billing nav-item (frontend/user-dashboard.html:894-896). Verified 2026-07-08.

navigation.evidence: frontend/user-dashboard.html:894-896 sidebar nav-item (data-page="billing") links to the billing flow, which forwards to frontend/billing-portal.html.

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/userPlanKeyLimit.test.mjs and workers/test/monthlyQuota.test.mjs import from workers/src/handlers/subscription.js; workers/test/emailWiringTask3Phase1.test.mjs covers workers/src/services/emailEngine.js, which workers/src/handlers/payments.js:1104 calls (sendPaymentFailedEmail) on a failed Razorpay payment webhook — confirmed by direct import-statement grep, 2026-07-08. workers/test/subscriptionLegacyRouteDelegation.test.mjs (new, 2026-07-10, 7 tests) and workers/test/paymentVerifyOrderIntegrity.test.mjs (new, 2026-07-10, 5 tests) — see notes below.

verification.evidence: frontend/billing-portal.html and frontend/user-dashboard.html:2760-2775,894-896 read directly; workers/src/handlers/subscription.js exports enumerated; workers/src/handlers/payments.js:1104 read to confirm sendPaymentFailedEmail is actually invoked, not just defined. 2026-07-10: workers/src/handlers/payments.js handleVerifyPayment and workers/src/handlers/subscription.js handleCreateSubscription/handleActivateSubscription re-read in full for the P0 Wave 2 payment/subscription audit — see notes. Post-deploy production verification (2026-07-10, PR #144, merge commit 2286b998, Deploy to Cloudflare run 29072659970 conclusion=success): direct curl calls against the live cyberdudebivash.in Worker confirm the new code is what's actually serving — POST /api/subscription/create with no email now returns the new 'A valid email is required to create a subscription order.' 400 (a check that did not exist pre-fix); with plan:'GOLD' now returns 'Invalid plan. Choose STARTER, PRO, ENTERPRISE, or MSSP.' (the pre-fix message omitted MSSP, so this exact wording is new-code-only evidence, not a coincidence); POST /api/subscription/activate with an empty body still 400s cleanly; POST /api/payments/verify with a malformed order_id is still correctly rejected before reaching the new authoritative-lookup code. This is dynamic_api evidence (real HTTP calls against live production), not dynamic_browser (no purchase was actually completed — would require a real Razorpay payment) — customer_journey_complete stays false pending an end-to-end purchase click-through.

notes: No actionable gap found in the 2026-07-08 pass (backend, frontend, navigation, tests, and docs all checked out) — P7 assigned by elimination, not because documentation is thin. Two items remain explicitly unverified rather than confirmed either way: renewal/dunning cron completeness and usage-based/API billing enforcement completeness (docs/SAAS_PRODUCTIZATION_MISSION_BRIEF.md:126). Flagged here for a future verification cycle rather than asserted working or broken. FIXED 2026-07-10 (P0 Wave 2, PR #144, merged + deployed, own regression tests) — two real findings from a full payment/subscription audit, both closed in this pass: (1) handleVerifyPayment (workers/src/handlers/payments.js) trusted the client-resent module/target/plan/product_id fields at verify time instead of the D1 payments row's own server-set values from order-creation — a customer could pay for the cheapest subscription tier/report/package and then call verify a second time claiming a more expensive one, reusing the same genuinely-signed order (the signature only proves order_id+payment_id are linked, not which plan was paid for). Fixed by making the D1 payments row authoritative for module/target/plan/product_id once it exists, client values now only a fallback when no matching row exists. (2) handleCreateSubscription/handleActivateSubscription (workers/src/handlers/subscription.js) — POST /api/subscription/create and POST /api/subscription/activate — were a second, parallel order-creation/verification implementation whose D1 writes used column names that don't exist in the live schema (order_id/payment_id vs. razorpay_order_id/razorpay_payment_id; processor/external_id vs. the real subscriptions schema), silently swallowed by .catch(), so every activation charged the customer and never granted access or touched users.tier at all — see PR #142's incident writeup, which fixed the one known frontend caller (the dashboard 'Upgrade to Pro' button) but left these routes themselves live, broken, and still publicly advertised in the /api index for any other caller. Rather than delete the routes (backward compatibility), both functions now delegate internally to the canonical, now-hardened handleCreateOrder/handleVerifyPayment (payments.js) via an internal synthetic-Request pattern already established elsewhere in payments.js (its own scan-report branch calls SCAN_HANDLERS the same way) — same architecture the master prompt's Phase 3 asks for ('every payment entry point should use the same orchestration layer'). Old response shapes preserved for backward compatibility. Regression coverage: workers/test/paymentVerifyOrderIntegrity.test.mjs (5 tests, real node:sqlite — subscription/package/scan-report tamper attempts all neutralized to the authoritative order, legitimate flow and DB-outage fallback both unaffected) and workers/test/subscriptionLegacyRouteDelegation.test.mjs (7 tests — full create→pay→activate round trip proves a real users.tier grant + JWT, which never happened before this fix; a tampered-plan activate attempt is neutralized via the inherited order-integrity fix; route-wiring contract check). Full backend suite green: 194 files / 2055 tests (192/2043 baseline + 2 new files / 12 tests, zero regressions). CI: a CodeQL high-severity finding ('incomplete URL substring sanitization' in the new test file's fetch stub — url.includes() instead of a real hostname check) was caught on the first CI run, fixed same-session, and re-verified green before merge — every one of the 32 required/advisory checks passed before merge, none bypassed. Not done this pass (disclosed, real remaining gaps from the same audit, each independently bounded): refunds ARE implemented (workers/src/handlers/v24Handler.js, owner-gated, real Razorpay refund API call) but have no admin UI beyond raw API calls; webhook_events (idempotency ledger) has no admin-facing viewer; invoice/receipt retrieval (workers/src/services/v24/billingEngine.js createInvoice) has no customer-facing GET/download endpoint found in this pass — customers currently only receive invoices by email attachment, not via a billing-portal download link.
